title: Styling Text and Custom Fonts
description: Go beyond basics: custom fonts, text shadows, and advanced typography.

slides.title: Advanced Text Styling РюЇ№ИЈ
slides.content:
# More Text Styles

Beyond basic `fontSize` and `color`, you can use:

- `fontFamily`: set a custom font.
- `fontWeight`: 'normal', 'bold', or numeric 100РђЉ900.
- `fontStyle`: 'normal', 'italic'.
- `letterSpacing`: space between characters.
- `lineHeight`: vertical space between lines.
- `textAlign`: 'auto', 'left', 'right', 'center', 'justify'.
- `textDecorationLine`: 'none', 'underline', 'lineРђЉthrough', 'underline lineРђЉthrough'.
- `textShadowColor`, `textShadowOffset`, `textShadowRadius`: add shadows.

slides.title: Custom Fonts Example

quizData.question: What do you need to load custom fonts in an Expo app?

options.text: Nothing, just place the font in assets

quizData.explanation: Expo requires `expo-font` to load custom fonts; `useFonts` handles loading state.

slides.title: Nested Text and Inheritance
slides.content:
## Style Inheritance

Text styles cascade from parent `<Text>` to child `<Text>`. This is useful for combining styles.

```jsx
<Text style={{ color: 'blue', fontSize: 16 }}>
  Blue text, 
  <Text style={{ fontWeight: 'bold' }}>bold blue</Text>
</Text>
```

Note: Only text styles inherit; layout properties do not.

slides.title: Pro Tip ­Ъџђ
slides.content:
Use `numberOfLines` and `ellipsizeMode` to truncate long text:
```jsx
<Text numberOfLines={2} ellipsizeMode="tail">
  This is a very long text that will be truncated after two lines.
</Text>
```
